title: Ahmedabad Geography - Location, Terrain & Climate
content:
Ahmedabad is a city located in the state of Gujarat in western India, known for its rich cultural heritage and historical significance. 

[FACTBOX]
Land Area: 471 km², about the size of the city of Chicago
Highest Point: None, as Ahmedabad is a city located on the plains
Lowest Point: None, as Ahmedabad is a city located on the plains
Coastline: landlocked
Borders: surrounded by other cities and towns of Gujarat state
Climate Type: tropical
[/FACTBOX]

[SECTION] Location & Borders [/SECTION]
Ahmedabad is situated in the state of Gujarat, which is located in the western part of India. The city is positioned on the banks of the **Sabarmati River**, which flows through the city and provides a source of water for the inhabitants. Ahmedabad is surrounded by other cities and towns of Gujarat state, including Gandhinagar, the state capital, which is located about 23 kilometers north of Ahmedabad. The city is also close to the **Gulf of Khambhat**, which is a major inlet of the Arabian Sea.

The location of Ahmedabad has played a significant role in its development as a major commercial and cultural center. The city's proximity to the sea and its position on the trade routes between India and the Middle East has made it an important center for trade and commerce. Ahmedabad is also connected to other major cities of India, including Mumbai, Delhi, and Bangalore, by road, rail, and air, making it an important transportation hub.

[SECTION] Terrain & Landscape [/SECTION]
The terrain of Ahmedabad is generally flat, with the city located on the plains of the **Sabarmati River**. The city's landscape is dominated by the river, which flows through the center of the city and provides a source of water for the inhabitants. The surrounding area is mostly agricultural land, with crops such as cotton, tobacco, and wheat being grown. The city itself is a mix of old and new, with ancient mosques, temples, and other historical buildings standing alongside modern skyscrapers and shopping malls.

One of the notable features of Ahmedabad's landscape is the presence of several **lakes and reservoirs**, which provide a source of water for the city. The city is also home to several **parks and gardens**, which provide a green oasis in the midst of the bustling city. The **Sabarmati Ashram**, which was the residence of Mahatma Gandhi during the Indian independence movement, is also located in Ahmedabad and is a major tourist attraction.

The city's terrain is also influenced by its location in a **seismic zone**, which makes it prone to earthquakes. The city has a hot and dry climate, with very little rainfall during the year. The climate is characterized by hot summers and mild winters, with the temperature ranging from 12°C to 45°C throughout the year.

[SECTION] Climate By Season [/SECTION]
[TABLE]
| Season | Months | Temperature Range | Rainfall | Conditions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Winter | December to February | 12°C to 25°C | minimal | cool and dry |
| Summer | March to May | 25°C to 45°C | minimal | hot and dry |
| Monsoon | June to September | 25°C to 35°C | high | hot and humid |
| Autumn | October to November | 20°C to 30°C | minimal | warm and dry |
[/TABLE]

[SECTION] Best Time To Visit [/SECTION]
[CALLOUT] Best months: October to February. Why: the weather is cool and pleasant, making it ideal for sightseeing and outdoor activities. Avoid: May and June, as the heat can be unbearable, and July to September, as the city experiences heavy rainfall during the monsoon season. [/CALLOUT]

[SECTION] Natural Highlights [/SECTION]
- The **Nal Sarovar Bird Sanctuary**, which is a major wetland area and home to a wide variety of bird species, is located near Ahmedabad.
- The **Thol Bird Sanctuary**, which is another important wetland area and home to a wide variety of bird species, is also located near Ahmedabad.
- The **Sabarmati River**, which flows through the city and provides a source of water for the inhabitants, is a major natural feature of Ahmedabad.
- The **Ahmedabad Forest**, which is a protected area and home to a wide variety of flora and fauna, is located on the outskirts of the city.
- The **Gulf of Khambhat**, which is a major inlet of the Arabian Sea, is located near Ahmedabad and provides a source of seafood for the city.
- The **Little Rann of Kutch**, which is a unique saltwater marshland and home to a wide variety of flora and fauna, is located near Ahmedabad.
